meta_title: For Students: design with AI to Solve can't debug code (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Design with AI to Solve Debugging Roadblocks
content.seo_description: Master advanced debugging by leveraging AI design patterns. A professional case study using Antigravity and Claude Code to accelerate your student projects.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't just fixing syntax; it's a design failure. By shifting to 'Design with AI,' you treat code as a modular architecture rather than a monolithic wall. Use Antigravity to map logic flows and Claude Code to automate recursive refactoring, turning unfixable bugs into architectural improvements and scalable system components.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a terminal bleeding red error logs, the cursor blinking with mocking indifference. You’ve refactored, you’ve googled, and you’ve reached that familiar, cold realization: you don’t know why the code is broken. For a student, this is the 'valley of death' where projects go to die and confidence evaporates into thin air.

But here is the secret: the best engineers don't 'debug'—they re-architect. By leveraging AI-assisted design, you stop wrestling with individual lines and start orchestrating system flows. This case study transforms your debugging process from a frustrating hunt into a methodical, AI-driven engineering exercise that will elevate your portfolio to industry-ready standards.

steps.title: Map the Logic Flow with Antigravity
steps.description: Before fixing, visualize the state transition. Use Antigravity to generate a structural map of your failing module to identify where the logic diverges from your initial design intent.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the provided codebase and generate a Mermaid sequence diagram representing the state transitions of the authentication flow. Highlight potential race conditions or unhandled promise rejections.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Points
steps.description: Use Claude Code to create a diagnostic harness that traps the error in a sandbox environment, preventing the 'fix-one-break-two' cycle.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Based on the flow map, design a unit test suite that isolates the specific module causing the memory leak. Focus on edge cases involving null payloads.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --task 'Generate a test harness for auth.ts that isolates runtime errors'

steps.title: Refactor via Architectural Constraints
steps.description: Instead of patching the bug, force the AI to rewrite the module using SOLID principles, which naturally resolves the underlying technical debt.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the identified module to follow the Dependency Inversion principle. Decouple the database logic from the business logic.

steps.title: Automated Verification Loops
steps.description: Validate the fix by running the AI-generated test suite against the new architecture to ensure no regressions were introduced.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the refactored code and generate a verification script that tests the system against the original failure conditions identified in Step 1.

steps.title: Documentation as Code
steps.description: Finalize the fix by generating technical documentation that explains the architectural shift, ensuring you understand the 'why' for your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a README.md snippet detailing the bug, the architectural flaw, and the solution. Use professional engineering terminology.

pro_tips: Always define your 'Success State' in Antigravity before asking for a code fix; if the AI doesn't know the goal, it will just hallucinate patches.
Use Claude Code's '--diff' flag to manually review every line change; never blindly accept AI-generated code blocks.
If a bug persists, ask the AI to 'explain the system state in plain English'—often, the logic error becomes obvious when written in prose.
Treat your debug logs as telemetry data; feed them directly into Claude Code to identify patterns rather than looking for single lines.
Build a 'Knowledge Base' file where you store architectural decisions; feed this file to the AI in every session to maintain context consistency.
content.next_steps: After completing this workflow, integrate these debugging steps into your CI/CD pipeline so that your future projects are resilient from the first commit.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical framework to stop fearing errors and start mastering complex systems. Your portfolio is only as strong as your ability to ship reliable, well-architected code. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

Use Claude Code to create a diagnostic harness that traps the error in a sandbox environment, preventing the 'fix-one-break-two' cycle.

Validate the fix by running the AI-generated test suite against the new architecture to ensure no regressions were introduced.
Finalize the fix by generating technical documentation that explains the architectural shift, ensuring you understand the 'why' for your portfolio.
